title: Mespilus populifolia

Mespilus populifolia is native to parts of Europe and Western Asia and is often found growing in woodland edges and hedgerows. The plant is also known as the Philadelphus population or the Amelanchier population, but Mespilus populifolia is its more commonly used name. Its classification is further supported by its botanical characteristics, including its white flowers and red or yellow berries.
